name: Bigelow Aerospace
description: Bigelow Aerospace, founded by Robert Bigelow, is an American aerospace company that aimed to develop expandable space habitat technology. The company's initial focus was on creating inflatable space stations, offering a potentially cheaper and more voluminous alternative to traditional rigid structures. Proponents within the alternative science community sometimes view Bigelow Aerospace's endeavors as pushing the boundaries of human space exploration and potentially unlocking new possibilities for off-world colonization and resource utilization. Some even speculate about the company's possible involvement in classified projects or the acquisition of advanced technologies. Key evidence cited includes the company's numerous patents related to expandable habitats, its partnership with NASA, and its outspoken founder's interest in paranormal phenomena, which fuels speculation about hidden agendas. Mainstream perspectives acknowledge Bigelow Aerospace's innovative approach to space habitat design but emphasize the technical challenges and financial constraints associated with developing and deploying such structures. Critics point to the company's eventual cessation of operations in 2020 as an indication of the inherent difficulties in the commercial space habitat market. Academics highlight the company's contribution to the field of space architecture and its role in stimulating discussion about the future of human space settlement.
subtitle: Expandable space habitats and future colonization.

claims.claim: Expandable habitats are a cost-effective alternative to traditional space stations.
claims.evidence: Bigelow Aerospace's claims of lower launch costs and larger internal volume compared to ISS modules.
claims.criticism: Technical challenges in ensuring long-term durability and radiation shielding, as well as the high overall cost of space missions, negate initial cost savings.

claims.claim: Bigelow Aerospace possesses advanced, possibly classified, technologies.
claims.evidence: Robert Bigelow's interest in paranormal research and alleged connections to undisclosed government projects.
claims.criticism: Lack of concrete evidence and reliance on speculation and conjecture. Mainstream sources attribute Bigelow's success to conventional engineering and business practices.

claims.claim: Expandable habitats will be crucial for future space colonization.
claims.evidence: The need for large, habitable spaces for long-duration missions and off-world settlements.
claims.criticism: Numerous other challenges, such as radiation shielding, life support systems, and resource availability, need to be addressed before large-scale space colonization becomes feasible. Expandable habitats are only one piece of the puzzle.

keyFigures.name: James Oberg
keyFigures.role: Space journalist and critic of sensationalized space exploration claims

mythologyConnections: Visions of futuristic habitats echo science fiction themes derived from ancient myths of utopia and human transcendence.
The quest to colonize space resonates with the myth of humanity's destiny to explore and conquer new frontiers.

timeline.event: Genesis I and II inflatable modules launched
BEAM (Bigelow Expandable Activity Module) attached to the ISS
Bigelow Aerospace lays off staff and ceases operations

sources.author: Various
sources.work: Online forums and conspiracy theory websites
sources.title: Various
